content.value: Talking about family is a great way to practice numbers and classifiers. 'Family members' in Cantonese is literally 'at home people' (屋企人 - uk1 kei2 jan4).

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: nei5 jau5 mou5 mui4 mui2 aa3?
lines.translation: Do you have any younger sisters?

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: mou5 aa3. ngo5 jau5 loeng5 go3 go4 go1.
lines.translation: No. I have two older brothers.

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: o4, keoi5 dei6 hai6 m4 hai6 hok6 saang1 aa3?

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: wai2, nei5 uk1 kei2 jan4 hou2 maa3?

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: gei2 hou2 aa3. nei5 jau5 mou5 go4 go1 aa3?
lines.translation: Quite good. Do you have any older brothers?

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: jau5 aa3. ngo5 jau5 jat1 go3 go4 go1.
lines.translation: Yes. I have one older brother.

pages.title: Family Mastered!
pages.summary: You can now describe your family and use classifiers to count people correctly.
